input.comment: constructor() - caller becomes the sole signer (count=1, required=1)
[on-chain] approve(0) before anything is queued - OperationNotFound, must revert
[on-chain] reject(0) before anything is queued - OperationNotFound, must revert

[on-chain] queue(AddSigner(0x...dead)) - signer queues an AddSigner op as op 0. TODO: solcore dispatch needs ABIEncode/SigString instances for sum types (Operation); this calldata is the canonical-Solidity-style placeholder for selector keccak256('queue(Operation)')[:4]=4176b43a + Operation tag 0 (AddSigner) + addr argument.
[on-chain] approve(0) - the lone signer approves their queued AddSigner. Approvals(0) -> Approvals(1) which meets the required threshold of 1.
[on-chain] approve(0) again from the same signer - SignerAlreadyApproved, must revert
[on-chain] execute(0, "") - anyone can execute the now-approved AddSigner. After this 0x...dead is signer 1, signers_count=2, nonce=1.
[on-chain] queue(AddSigner(0x...cafe)) - signer queues a second AddSigner op as op 1, to be rejected by the test below.
[on-chain] reject(1) - lone signer rejects the AddSigner. Approvals(0) -> Rejected.
[on-chain] approve(1) after reject - status is Rejected, UnexpectedStatus, must revert
[on-chain] execute(1, "") - rejected ops are skipped (no-op execute) and nonce advances to 2.

[off-chain] queueWithSignature(AddSigner(0x...beef), Contract(signer)) - anyone may call this; signature is a Contract-signature variant referencing the lone signer's caller address, which has pre-approved the operation hash via ApproveSignedHash. NOTE: requires ABIEncode/SigString for Operation+Signature and a prior ApproveSignedHash op; replace placeholder bytes once dispatch supports sum types.
[off-chain] approveWithSignature(2, Contract(signer)) - approve op 2 via signature path. Placeholder calldata: selector + nonce(2) + signature ADT bytes (TBD).

[off-chain] rejectWithSignature(2, Contract(signer)) - status is already Approvals(1), so this would trip SignerAlreadyApproved/UnexpectedStatus from the signature path. Demonstrates the off-chain reject surface; for a clean reject use a fresh op (see below).
[off-chain] queueWithSignature(AddSigner(0x...f00d), Contract(signer)) - queue op 3, to be rejected below.
[off-chain] rejectWithSignature(3, Contract(signer)) - off-chain reject of the freshly queued AddSigner. Approvals(0) -> Rejected.
